perturbation.original_text: 1.6 Payment. If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 Payment. If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. However, under special circumstances as determined by the Trust's board, the Investment Advisor will pay in 60 days. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that under special circumstances, determined by the Trust's board, the Investment Advisor will pay in 60 days. This contradicts the previous statement in the same section specifying that the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days. The uncertainty introduced by this contradiction creates ambiguity regarding the payment deadline, leading to potential disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 Payment. If the Investment Adviser is required to reimburse the Fund, the Investment Adviser shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end in the amount due the Fund as of each month. However, under special circumstances as determined by the Trust's board, the Investment Advisor will pay in 60 days. If the Fund is required to pay the Investment Adviser the Expense Waiver, the Fund shall make such payment within 30 days after each month-end. Reimbursements to the Fund or the Expense Waiver shall not include any additional charges or fees whatsoever, including, e.g., interest accruable on such reimbursements or the Expense Waiver.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that under special circumstances, the Investment Advisor can pay in 60 days, directly conflicting with the initial statement in the same section that payment shall be made within 30 days. This creates uncertainty and a direct conflict regarding the payment deadline, leading to potential disputes. As per the provided definitions, this falls under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting deadlines.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement, or after 5 years, whichever comes first, except the Investment Adviser's Right of Recovery set forth in section 1.5 herein.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a contradiction by specifying that the agreement will terminate upon the termination of the Advisory Agreement or after 5 years, whichever comes first. This creates an in-text contradiction with section 3, where it is stated that the agreement will continue in effect for a period of one year from the date of its execution and from year to year thereafter provided such continuance is specifically approved. Now the agreement can either be extended yearly or teminated after 5 years, so it is contradictory
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue in effect for a period of one year from the date of its execution and from year to year thereafter provided such continuance is specifically approved by a majority of the trustees of the Trust who (i) are not "interested persons" of the Trust or any other party to this Agreement, as defined in the 1940 Act, and (ii) have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of this Agreement ("Non- Interested Trustees").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a termination condition (after 5 years) that directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which specifies that the agreement continues from year to year with specific approval. The modified text allows for termination after a fixed period, regardless of trustee approval, creating a contradiction. The inconsistency creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration, making enforcement unclear, as the agreement can be either extended yearly or terminated after 5 years, so it is contradictory.
